metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how `scope-intent` gets its own `ticket_context` (the union ticket scope) when all other batch-run overlays share the single `CodeReviewBatchRunner._context`; concretely: run it as a standalone agent step wired directly in the YAML (bypassing the batch runner), or extend `CodeReviewBatchRunner` with a per-overlay context map.
findings.finding: The `CodeReviewBatchRunner` constructs every overlay step with the same single `_context` string (`inputs={'ticket_context': self._context}` — `batch_runner.py:58`). The plan requires the `scope-intent` overlay to receive a different `ticket_context` (the union ticket scope) while all other overlays get the diff context. The batch runner's architecture (one `_context` shared across all criteria in a batch) is in direct conflict with this per-overlay context requirement. The plan does not specify how to resolve this tension — whether to (a) modify `CodeReviewBatchRunner` to accept a per-overlay context map, (b) run `scope-intent` as a standalone agent step (outside the batch runner), or (c) some other mechanism. Without this, the ticket-blindness guarantee for other overlays cannot be met through the batch runner path.
findings.location: Data-flow section / `src/rebar/llm/code_review/batch_runner.py:58`
findings.suggested_fix: Run `scope-intent` as a standalone agent step in `code-review.yaml` (parallel to the `base` step), giving it `with: {ticket_context: ${{ steps.assemble_diff.outputs.ticket_scope_context }}}` where `ticket_scope_context` is the new output from `assemble_diff`. This keeps the batch runner unchanged, provides clean context isolation, and makes ticket-blindness of other overlays trivially testable (they receive no `ticket_scope_context` input).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete isolation mechanism: e.g., use a distinct output key name (e.g., `scope_context` rather than overloading `ticket_context`) so ordinary overlays never receive it, OR describe how `CodeReviewBatchRunner` is modified to conditionally inject per-overlay context.
findings.finding: The ticket-isolation protocol ('gate ticket context to the scope-intent overlay only; do not leak it to the others') is referenced as a required mechanism but is never specified — `CodeReviewBatchRunner` currently injects `ticket_context: self._context` uniformly for ALL overlays, and the plan names no concrete change to alter this.
findings.location: What section: 'Ticket-blindness is NOT free … gate ticket context to the scope-intent overlay only'; Data-flow section; src/rebar/llm/code_review/batch_runner.py:58
findings.suggested_fix: Introduce a new `assemble_diff` output key `scope_context` (separate from `context`/`ticket_context`), wire it only to the scope-intent step via an explicit `with: scope_context: ${{ steps.assemble_diff.outputs.scope_context }}`, and have the scope-intent prompt use `{{scope_context}}` rather than `{{ticket_context}}`. This keeps all other overlays (which receive `ticket_context` from the batch runner's `self._context`) completely unaware of ticket data.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `CodeReviewBatchRunner` to the edit scope and specify how it gains per-overlay context injection (e.g., an optional `overlay_context_overrides: dict[str, str]` param) so that only scope-intent receives ticket union context while base and other overlays keep the diff-only context.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'ticket_context is threaded into the shared reviewer_input, so keeping base + other overlays ticket-blind requires explicit plumbing' — but the plan never calls out that `CodeReviewBatchRunner.run()` is an INDEPENDENT injection site that also supplies `ticket_context` uniformly to every overlay (batch_runner.py line 58: `inputs={"ticket_context": self._context, ...}`). Achieving per-overlay context isolation requires modifying `CodeReviewBatchRunner`'s interface (e.g., a per-overlay context override map), which the plan does not name as a required change.
findings.location: Data-flow section / What section ('gate ticket context to the scope-intent overlay only') / src/rebar/llm/code_review/batch_runner.py line 58
findings.suggested_fix: Add `CodeReviewBatchRunner.__init__` an optional `overlay_context_overrides: dict[str, str] = {}` parameter; in `run()`, replace `self._context` with `overlay_context_overrides.get(prompt_id, self._context)`. The scope-intent overlay's prompt_id would be mapped to the ticket union context. `gate_dispatch.produce_code_review_verdict` constructs the runner with this override populated only when trailer tickets resolve.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the unverified capability claim: describe concretely how `CodeReviewBatchRunner` will be extended to support a different `ticket_context` for `scope-intent` vs. all other overlays (e.g., a `per_overlay_context: dict[str, str]` constructor arg, a sentinel value, or a separate runner instance).
findings.finding: The plan asserts that ticket context can be 'gated to the scope-intent overlay only' inside the batch runner, but this is an unverified capability claim: CodeReviewBatchRunner today injects one uniform context string into every overlay's ticket_context with no per-prompt-id differentiation mechanism.
findings.location: What section — 'gate ticket context to the scope-intent overlay only'; AC3 — 'A test proves base + other overlays receive NO ticket context (ticket-blind)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/code_review/batch_runner.py` to the Scope edit targets and specify how `CodeReviewBatchRunner` differentiates the scope-intent overlay's `ticket_context` from the shared diff context injected to all other overlays.
findings.finding: `CodeReviewBatchRunner` (`src/rebar/llm/code_review/batch_runner.py`) is an unenumerated edit target. It constructs every overlay's `StepContext` with a single `ticket_context: self._context` (the diff context, line 58), making it the mechanism that would need to change to inject a different (ticket-scope) context exclusively for the scope-intent overlay while keeping all others ticket-blind.
findings.location: `src/rebar/llm/code_review/batch_runner.py:58` / Data-flow section ('gate ticket context to the scope-intent overlay only')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the new `assemble_diff_output` field (e.g., `ticket_union_context`) that carries the assembled scope/AC union string, add it to `assemble_diff_output.schema.json`, and show its YAML reference in the scope-intent overlay step's `ticket_context` input.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'assemble_diff_input schema: add optional `commit_message`' but the current `assemble_diff_output.schema.json` only declares `context` and `changed_files` properties. The new ticket union context must be emitted as a SEPARATE output field (distinct from `context`) so the scope-intent overlay's `ticket_context` is wired to it in the YAML — yet the plan never names this new output field or specifies that `assemble_diff_output.schema.json` must be updated.
findings.location: Data-flow section ('exposed as a SEPARATE context field consumed ONLY by the scope-intent overlay') / src/rebar/schemas/assemble_diff_output.schema.json
findings.suggested_fix: Add `"ticket_union_context": {"type": "string", "default": ""}` to `assemble_diff_output.schema.json`. In `code-review.yaml`, the scope-intent overlay step receives `ticket_context: ${{ steps.assemble_diff.outputs.ticket_union_context }}` while all other steps keep `ticket_context: ${{ steps.assemble_diff.outputs.context }}`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify exactly where in the Gerrit voter pipeline the commit message is retrieved (e.g., from the Gerrit change object already in scope), trace the full call chain from `voter.py` → `adapter.code_decision` → `produce_code_review_verdict`, and add `commit_message` to each intermediate signature.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'Call sites (`shim.review_code`, `review_bot/voter.py`): pass the change's commit message when available (Gerrit change commit body), default "" otherwise' — but reading `adapter.py` (the actual voter) shows `produce_code_review_verdict` is called with only `diff_text`, `repo_root`, and `enabled=True` (lines 186-191); there is no Gerrit commit-message retrieval at that call site. The plan does not specify WHERE in the Gerrit voter pipeline the commit message is sourced from.
findings.location: Data-flow section ('Call sites ... pass the change's commit message when available (Gerrit change commit body)') / src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py lines 186-191

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the new `assemble_diff` output key for the union ticket context (e.g., `scope_context`) and add it to `assemble_diff_output.schema.json`.
findings.finding: The new `assemble_diff` output key that carries the union ticket context to the scope-intent overlay is never named — the YAML wiring expression `${{ steps.assemble_diff.outputs.??? }}` cannot be written without it, and `assemble_diff_output.schema.json` is not updated.
findings.location: Data-flow section: 'exposed as a SEPARATE context field consumed ONLY by the scope-intent overlay'; src/rebar/schemas/assemble_diff_output.schema.json

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify where the '≥1 ticket resolved' content trigger gate lives: e.g., a boolean output key from `assemble_diff` (e.g., `has_ticket_context: bool`) consumed by a `when:` on the scope-intent step in code-review.yaml.
findings.finding: The content-trigger decision rule for scope-intent ('fires only when ≥1 trailer ticket resolves') has no specified placement: whether it lives in `overlay_union`, a new step, a `when:` expression in `code-review.yaml`, or the `assemble_diff` step itself.
findings.location: AC1: 'overlay fires only when a ticket resolves from the trailer'; Data-flow section; src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/code-review.yaml
findings.suggested_fix: Have `assemble_diff` emit a `has_scope_context: bool` (true iff ≥1 trailer ticket resolved), then add the scope-intent step to code-review.yaml with `when: ${{ steps.assemble_diff.outputs.has_scope_context }}`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `gate_dispatch.py`, `shim.review_code` / `review_bot/voter.py` (and `gates/code-review.yaml`) to the Scope section, and add an AC verifying that the commit message is threaded through to the overlay in the live call path.
findings.finding: The Data-flow section describes required changes to `gate_dispatch.py`, `shim.review_code`, and `review_bot/voter.py` as call-site updates, but none of these files appear in the Scope section, creating a cross-section contradiction between the change set described and the files declared in scope.
findings.location: Data-flow section vs. Scope section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify `reviewers/code-review-scope-intent.md`: frontmatter (inputs, outputs/output_schema, dimension, execution_mode), the criteria ids the LLM must use (drawn from or adapted from plan-review's F4/E3/G5), and the evidence-record contract / finding format.
findings.finding: `reviewers/code-review-scope-intent.md` is listed as the primary deliverable but does not exist and its prompt structure is never specified: no frontmatter (inputs, outputs, dimension, output_schema, execution_mode), no variable declarations, no criteria vocabulary the LLM must use, and no finding-format instruction.
findings.location: Scope section: 'reviewers/code-review-scope-intent.md'; src/rebar/llm/reviewers/ (directory listing)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Prompt spec' subsection to the plan stating: dimension: `scope-intent`, output_schema: `code_review_findings`, criteria vocabulary: `["scope-drift"]` (or whichever specific ids), and a template modeled on code-review-security.md with the union ticket scope/AC block injected via `{{ticket_context}}`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `{ prompt: code-review-scope-intent, when: "${{ steps.triggers.outputs.include_scope_intent }}" }` to `round_a` and `{ prompt: code-review-scope-intent, when: "${{ steps.union.outputs.include_scope_intent }}" }` to `round_b` in `gates/code-review.yaml`, and add these to the Scope edit targets.
findings.finding: The `round_a` and `round_b` batch criteria lists in `gates/code-review.yaml` hardcode exactly the 11 current overlay ids. Adding `scope-intent` to `OVERLAY_IDS` without adding a corresponding `when:` entry to both lists means `overlay_union` will emit `include_scope_intent` but the overlay prompt will never execute.
findings.location: `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/code-review.yaml` lines 72–82 (round_a) and 100–110 (round_b)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `reviewers/code-review-base.md` to the Scope edit targets and update its overlay catalog bullet list to include `scope-intent —` so `test_base_prompt_enumerates_exactly_the_overlay_ids` continues to pass.
findings.finding: `code-review-base.md` is not listed as an edit target, but adding `scope-intent` to `OVERLAY_IDS` will immediately break `test_base_prompt_enumerates_exactly_the_overlay_ids`, which asserts the prompt body enumerates EXACTLY the OVERLAY_IDS catalog using a `- \`<id>\` —` bullet pattern.
findings.location: Scope section / `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-base.md` / `tests/unit/test_code_review_recommend_overlays.py:149`
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `- \`scope-intent\` — scope/intent alignment between the diff and its referenced ticket(s).` bullet to the overlay catalog section in `code-review-base.md`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the required `scope-intent` entry shape in `criteria_routing.json` (at minimum: `exec`, `applies_to`, `default_posture`, `block_threshold`, `blocking_enabled`) so the `test_code_review_ws2` routing-parity guard passes.
findings.finding: `criteria_routing.json` (`src/rebar/llm/code_review/criteria_routing.json`) is listed as a Scope edit target but the plan does not specify what entry must be added for `scope-intent`. The existing test `test_code_review_ws2.py:22` asserts `oid in idx` for every OVERLAY_IDS member — adding `scope-intent` to OVERLAY_IDS without a matching routing entry will break this test.
findings.location: Scope section (`criteria_routing.json (SHARED — coordinate with C, F)`) / `tests/unit/test_code_review_ws2.py:22`
findings.suggested_fix: Add `"scope-intent": {"exec": "AGENT", "applies_to": [], "default_posture": "advisory", "block_threshold": 0.95, "blocking_enabled": false}` — empty `applies_to` because the trigger is content-based (trailer), not glob-based.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC1) that verifies the scope-intent overlay fires when a real commit_message with a valid ticket trailer is supplied through the voter.py / shim call path — e.g., an integration test that invokes produce_code_review_verdict with a commit_message containing a trailer and asserts the overlay is included in the output.
findings.finding: The plan threads commit_message through five new call sites (assemble_diff_context, assemble_diff_input schema, gates/code-review.yaml, gate_dispatch.produce_code_review_verdict, shim.review_code / voter.py) but no acceptance criterion exercises this full end-to-end plumbing path — only the ticket-blindness isolation (AC3) is tested. A defect anywhere in the threading chain (e.g., commit_message silently dropped in gate_dispatch or voter.py) would leave the overlay permanently non-firing with no AC to catch it.
findings.location: Data-flow section; Acceptance Criteria (gap — no AC covers call-site threading)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace '(eval)' in AC4 with a concrete proving command — e.g., a pytest fixture that feeds a two-ticket commit message and a diff touching both tickets' declared scope, then asserts the overlay emits no scope-drift finding (or asserts advisory-only output).
findings.finding: AC4 ('A multi-ticket commit naming all its tickets produces no false scope-drift finding (eval)') qualifies its proving mechanism only as '(eval)', with no concrete test structure, command, or fixture described. It is unclear whether 'eval' means an automated unit/integration test, a deterministic CI command, or a manual LLM evaluation pass — making the criterion neither concretely verifiable nor deterministically in-session completable.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Add a pytest parametrize test in the overlay's test module: given commit_message with two ticket trailers whose union scope covers all diff hunks, assert the scope-intent overlay returns no drift findings. This is deterministic, in-session, and directly exercises the union-context assembly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Justify the batch-member approach for `scope-intent` over the standalone-step approach (identical to how `base` is already a standalone step that gets its own `ticket_context` wiring via YAML `with:`) — or pivot to the standalone-step pattern if it avoids the per-overlay context routing complexity.
findings.finding: The ticket-context isolation design (gating a different ticket_context to scope-intent while keeping all other overlays ticket-blind) is a novel architectural pattern with no codebase prior art, but the plan does not compare it against the obvious established alternative: running scope-intent as a dedicated standalone agent step (parallel to the existing 'base' step) rather than as a batch member requiring bespoke per-overlay-instance context routing.
findings.location: What section — 'gate ticket context to the scope-intent overlay only; do not leak it to the others'; Data-flow section
findings.suggested_fix: Run scope-intent as a standalone YAML step (`id: scope_intent`, `needs: [assemble_diff]`, `prompt: code-review-scope-intent`, `with: { ticket_context: ${{ steps.assemble_diff.outputs.scope_context }} }`) triggered by a `when:` guard on `steps.assemble_diff.outputs.has_ticket_context`. This avoids modifying CodeReviewBatchRunner entirely and uses the existing established pattern for steps needing differentiated inputs.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or named eval case) asserting that a diff containing changes clearly outside the ticket's stated scope produces at least one advisory scope-intent finding, to validate the true-positive detection path.
findings.finding: The test plan covers ticket-blind isolation (AC3) and a no-false-positive multi-ticket eval (AC4), but there is no acceptance criterion for the positive-detection case: a diff that genuinely drifts outside the ticket's scope DOES trigger an advisory finding. Without a true-positive test, the overlay's core detection capability is unvalidated and could silently produce zero findings for all inputs while passing all ACs.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3, AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Add: '- [ ] An eval case where the diff introduces changes unrelated to any referenced ticket's scope produces ≥1 advisory scope-drift finding from the scope-intent overlay.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the data-flow section that `voter.py` should extract the commit message from the `commit_info` already fetched at line 365 (e.g. `commit_info.get('message', '')`), and name this as an explicit edit to `voter.py`.
findings.finding: The plan names `review_bot/voter.py` as a call site that must 'pass the change's commit message when available (Gerrit change commit body)', but `voter.py` does not currently extract the commit message from Gerrit — it calls `gc.get_commit()` for parent-count detection (line 365) but the commit body is not surfaced or passed. The plan does not enumerate which `GerritClient` method supplies the message or where in `voter.py` the extraction occurs.
findings.location: Data-flow section ('Call sites ... review_bot/voter.py: pass the change's commit message when available') / `src/rebar/review_bot/voter.py:365`
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the data-flow: 'In `voter.py`, extract `commit_message = commit_info.get("message", "")` from the existing `gc.get_commit` result (already fetched for merge detection) and pass it through `adapter.code_review_decision` → `produce_code_review_verdict`.' Also update `adapter.code_review_decision` signature in the edit-target list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the fallback for partial or total ticket resolution failure: e.g., 'overlay fires only with the successfully-resolved tickets' scope; if zero resolve, overlay does not fire (treat as no trailer).'
findings.finding: No fallback is specified for the sub-step where `extract_ticket_refs` finds IDs but ticket resolution fails (e.g., network error, unknown alias, private ticket) — the plan states no behavior for partial or total resolution failure.
findings.location: Data-flow section: 'for each resolved trailer ticket it loads that ticket's scope/AC'; What section: 'assemble the referenced tickets' scope/AC as this overlay's context'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the ticket-context isolation contract (scope-intent overlay is the sole recipient of ticket context; all other overlays must remain ticket-blind) in CLAUDE.md, an ADR, or a design-doc section so future overlay contributors have an authoritative reference.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel architectural decision — gating ticket context exclusively to the scope-intent overlay while keeping all other overlays ticket-blind — but does not call for capturing this design in an ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design-doc update.
findings.location: ## Scope / Data-flow section (ticket-blind gating pattern)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a brief ADR or a CLAUDE.md section titled 'Ticket-context isolation' that states: (1) only the scope-intent overlay receives ticket context, (2) the test in AC #3 enforces this, and (3) any new overlay must be verified against this invariant.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that verifies `reviewers/code-review-scope-intent.md` exists and contains the required scope-intent criterion vocabulary (e.g., reusing plan-review's scope-intent terms as stated in 'What').
findings.finding: The new overlay file `reviewers/code-review-scope-intent.md` is listed in both the 'What' and 'Scope' sections as the primary deliverable, but no Acceptance Criterion covers its creation, required structure, or the criterion vocabulary it must contain.
findings.location: Scope section; AC list
findings.suggested_fix: Add: '- [ ] `reviewers/code-review-scope-intent.md` exists and its criteria section includes scope-intent vocabulary aligned with plan-review's scope-intent criterion (e.g., a snapshot test or content-contains assertion).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale explaining why `commit_message` is threaded as a new parameter all the way through `shim.review_code` → `produce_code_review_verdict`, vs. extracting it at the voter from the already-fetched `commit_info['message']` (lower blast radius, no API surface change on `shim.review_code`).
findings.finding: The plan does not state a positive rationale for threading `commit_message` end-to-end as a new parameter versus extracting it from the `CommitInfo` already fetched in `voter.py` for merge detection. `voter.py:365` calls `gc.get_commit(change_id, revision)` and stores the result in `commit_info`; Gerrit's `CommitInfo` response includes a `message` field containing the commit message. The plan proposes adding `commit_message` as a parameter to `review_code`, `produce_code_review_verdict`, `adapter.code_review_decision`, and `voter.py` — but it does not explain why threading it as a parameter is preferred over reading `commit_info['message']` at the voter level (where it's already available) and passing it into the existing `code_review_decision` call. Without a positive rationale, an implementer adding the new REST call path from scratch incurs unnecessary blast radius.
findings.location: Data-flow section / `src/rebar/review_bot/voter.py:365`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify error handling for ticket-resolution failure in assemble.py: define the fallback behavior (e.g., log a warning and treat the overlay as non-firing), and add at least one log/metric emission so operators can observe when ticket resolution fails.
findings.finding: The plan specifies graceful degradation when commit_message is absent (default '' → overlay does not fire), but does not address error handling when a trailer ticket ID is present yet resolution fails (ticket not found, malformed ID, or storage/load error).
findings.location: Data-flow section: 'for each resolved trailer ticket it loads that ticket's scope/AC'
findings.suggested_fix: In assemble_diff_context, wrap each ticket load in a try/except; on failure, log a structured warning (ticket_id, error) and exclude that ticket from the union context (if all fail, the overlay does not fire). Add an AC: 'If ticket resolution fails, the overlay is skipped and a warning is logged; the gate does not error.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Unify the field name for the assembled ticket scope context to a single term throughout the plan (e.g., `ticket_context`) and use it consistently in the Data-flow section, AC, and overlay template spec.
findings.finding: The plan uses two different terms for the same assembled artifact: 'ticket_context' (What section, AC) and 'union scope-context string' / 'UNION scope-context string' (Data-flow section), causing terminology drift that could lead to mismatched field names in implementation.
findings.location: 'What' section; Data-flow section; AC line 2
findings.suggested_fix: Pick `ticket_context` (already used in the AC and What section) and replace 'union scope-context string'/'UNION scope-context string' in the Data-flow section with `ticket_context`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add description and an AC covering what `criteria_routing.json` entry is needed for the scope-intent overlay and how to verify it (e.g., routing smoke test).
findings.finding: `criteria_routing.json` is listed in the Scope section as a file requiring changes (marked 'SHARED — coordinate with C, F'), but neither the plan body nor any AC describes what changes are needed, what routing rules are added, or how correctness is verified.
findings.location: Scope section: 'criteria_routing.json (SHARED — coordinate with C, F)'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
